---
title: "Check the model assumption"
format: html
---

# Check the model assumption: normal residuals

- Linear regression assumes normal residuals and continuous response.

```{r}

limma_rslt_files = c(
    list.files("../results/CellFie_Hu2025/Limma/", pattern="*.RDS", recursive=T, full.name=T) %>%
        grep("[0-9].RDS|result.RDS",.,value=T),
    list.files("../results/CellFie_Zhou2020/Limma/", pattern="*.RDS", recursive=T, full.name=T) %>%
        grep("[0-9].RDS|result.RDS",.,value=T)
)
folders = dirname(limma_rslt_files)
lapply(seq_along(limma_rslt_files), function(i) {
    file = limma_rslt_files[i]
    OUTDIR = folders[i]
    paste0(OUTDIR, "/modelCheck") %>% 
        dir.create(., recursive=T, showWarnings=FALSE)
    limma_results = readRDS(file)
    rslt = limma_results$rlst_list
    if (is.null(rslt)) {
        rslt = limma_results
    }
    pairs = names(rslt)
    lapply(pairs, function(pair) {
        fitted_vals = fitted(rslt[[pair]]$fitted.model)
        observed_vals = rslt[[pair]]$data
        res = observed_vals - fitted_vals
        pdf(paste0(OUTDIR, "/modelCheck/Test_residuals_normality_", pair, ".pdf"))
            padj = lapply(1:nrow(res), function(i) {
                x = as.numeric(res[i,])
                normT = ks.test(x, "pnorm", mean(x), sd(x))
                # normT = shapiro.test(x)
                fit_normal = fitdist(x, "norm")
                print(plot(fit_normal))
                return(normT$p.value )
            }) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric() %>% p.adjust(., method="BH")
            df = data.frame(Task=rownames(res), ksTestPadj=padj) %>%
                openxlsx::write.xlsx(
                    paste0(OUTDIR, "/modelCheck/Test_residuals_normality_", pair, ".xlsx"))
        dev.off()
    })
})



```
